ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Timepoint/visit label
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: Thinking about your hospital stay for your delivery, how satisfied were you with how you were treated by your care team, including nurses, doctors, and other staff? In this response, consider how much you felt you were treated with courtesy and respect, how clearly things were explained to you, and how well the care team listened to your concerns.
Notes: 1= Very dissatisfied; 2= Dissatisfied; 3= Satisfied; 4= Very satisfied;

ElementDescription: Was your support person present for you delivery?
Since hospital discharge have you been diagnosed with and/or started or restarted treatment for any mental health condition?
Notes: 0= No, I haven't; 1= Yes, I have

ElementDescription: Do you suspect you have any mental health conditions?
What is the status of your mental health treatment?
Notes: 1=I started/restarted therapy; 2=I started/restarted medication; 3=I started/restarted both therapy and medication; 4=I have not started/restarted either medication or therapy
